Mandatory: must immediately discontinue use
Recommended: SHOULD use DHCP to reacquire or verify its IP address and network parameters whenever the local network parameters may have changed

Mandatory: is required
should be able to
Recommended: should be able to

SHOULD: SHOULD use whatever information is available in the configuration information repository to choose an address to reuse
SHOULD probe the reused address before allocating the address
SHOULD probe the newly received address

Mandatory: MUST
REQUIRED
Recommended: SHOULD
RECOMMENDED

Mandatory: MUST NOT check for an existing lease
Recommended: SHOULD unicast the DHCPACK reply to the address given in the 'ciaddr' field of the DHCPINFORM message
SHOULD check the network address in a DHCPINFORM message for consistency

must be included: The first four octets of the 'options' field of the DHCP message contain the (decimal) values 99, 130, 83 and 99, respectively
One particular option - the 'DHCP message type' option - must be included in every DHCP message

Mandatory: MUST
MUST
Recommended: SHOULD

MUST NOT: MUST NOT send a lease expiration time

Mandatory: MUST insert its known network address as a 'requested IP address' option
MUST NOT include a 'server identifier' in the DHCPREQUEST message
Prohibited: MUST NOT include a 'server identifier' in the DHCPREQUEST message

mandatory: should not act as a DHCP server unless explicitly configured to do so by a system administrator
would preclude reliable operation if random hosts were allowed to respond to DHCP requests
cannot be guessed or assumed to have correct defaults
may not always be able to defend their network addresses
cannot be guaranteed to avoid allocation of duplicate network addresses
eliminates the necessity of having a DHCP server on each physical network segment

Mandatory: MUST use the same 'client identifier' in all subsequent messages
MUST use that identifier to identify the client
MUST use the contents of the 'chaddr' field to identify the client
is crucial for a DHCP client to use an identifier unique within the subnet
Recommended: SHOULD provide a mechanism for the user to select directly the 'vendor class identifier' values

Mandatory: The client unicasts DHCPRELEASE messages to the server
Because the client is declining the use of the IP address supplied by the server, the client broadcasts DHCPDECLINE messages
If the client receives no response to DHCP messages sent to the IP address of a known DHCP server, the DHCP client reverts to using the IP broadcast address
MUST be directed to the 'DHCP server' UDP port
Prohibited: SHOULD NOT request lease time parameters
SHOULD NOT display a message informing the user of the problem
Recommended: SHOULD display a message informing the user of the problem
SHOULD begin network processing using suitable defaults as per Appendix A
Not Recommended: SHOULD NOT request lease time parameters

Mandatory: MUST include that list in any subsequent DHCPREQUEST messages
must appear in the 'options' field of the message
Recommended: SHOULD include the 'maximum DHCP message size' option
SHOULD respond to the client with a DHCPNAK message
Conditional constraints: The client fills in the 'ciaddr' field only when correctly configured with an IP address in BOUND, RENEWING or REBINDING state
The 'requested IP address' option is to be filled in only in a DHCPREQUEST message when the client is verifying network parameters obtained previously

Mandatory: MUST
MUST
MUST
MUST be prepared to receive
MUST be prepared to receive
MUST be set to zero
MUST be ignored
Recommended: SHOULD accept

Mandatory: DHCP must allow local system administrators control over configuration parameters where desired
Clients should require no manual configuration
Each client should be able to discover appropriate local configuration parameters without user intervention
Networks should require no manual configuration for individual clients
DHCP should not require a server on each subnet
DHCP must work across routers or through the intervention of BOOTP relay agents
A DHCP client must be prepared to receive multiple responses to a request for configuration parameters
DHCP must coexist with statically configured, non-participating hosts and with existing network protocol implementations
DHCP must interoperate with the BOOTP relay agent behavior as described by RFC 951 and by RFC 1542
DHCP must provide service to existing BOOTP clients
DHCP must guarantee that any specific network address will not be in use by more than one DHCP client at a time
DHCP must retain DHCP client configuration across DHCP client reboot
DHCP must retain DHCP client configuration across server reboots
DHCP must allow automated assignment of configuration parameters to new clients to avoid hand configuration for new clients
DHCP must support fixed or permanent allocation of configuration parameters to specific clients
Recommended: local system administrators should be able to enforce local policies concerning allocation and access to local resources where desired
A DHCP client should, whenever possible, be assigned the same configuration parameters (e.g., network address) in response to each request
whenever possible, a DHCP client should be assigned the same configuration parameters despite restarts of the DHCP mechanism

Mandatory: The client MUST include its hardware address in the 'chaddr' field, if necessary for delivery of DHCP reply messages
If the client included a list of requested parameters in a DHCPDISCOVER message, the client MUST include that list in all subsequent messages
If the 'xid' of an arriving DHCPOFFER message does not match the 'xid' of the most recent DHCPDISCOVER message, the DHCPOFFER message must be silently discarded
Any arriving DHCPACK messages must be silently discarded
If the network address appears to be in use, the client MUST send a DHCPDECLINE message to the server
Recommended: The client SHOULD wait a random time between one and ten seconds to desynchronize the use of DHCP at startup
The client SHOULD perform a check on the suggested address to ensure that the address is not already in use
The client SHOULD broadcast an ARP reply to announce the client's new IP address and clear any outdated ARP cache entries in hosts on the client's subnet

Mandatory: MUST NOT fill in the 'ciaddr' field
MUST use the same 'client identifier'
MUST send a DHCPDECLINE message to the server
MUST broadcast the DHCPNAK message to the 0xffffffff broadcast address
MUST send the DHCPNAK message to the IP address of the BOOTP relay agent
Prohibited: MUST NOT fill in the 'ciaddr' field
Recommended: SHOULD NOT check that the client's network address is already in use
SHOULD respond with a DHCPNAK message to the client
SHOULD NOT respond if their information is not guaranteed to be accurate
SHOULD NOT respond with a DHCPNAK unless the servers are using an explicit mechanism to maintain coherency among the servers
Not Recommended: SHOULD NOT check that the client's network address is already in use
SHOULD NOT respond if their information is not guaranteed to be accurate
SHOULD NOT respond with a DHCPNAK unless the servers are using an explicit mechanism to maintain coherency among the servers

Mandatory: SHOULD be chosen as follows
MUST include that value in an appropriate option in the 'option' field
MUST include the default value for that parameter as given in the Host Requirements Document in an appropriate option in the 'option' field
MUST NOT return a value for that parameter
MUST supply as many of the requested parameters as possible
MUST omit any parameters it cannot provide
MUST include each requested parameter only once unless explicitly allowed in the DHCP Options and BOOTP Vendor Extensions document
MUST return to the client
Prohibited: SHOULD NOT reuse the selected network address before the client responds to the server's DHCPOFFER message
MUST NOT return a value for that parameter
Recommended: SHOULD be chosen as follows
SHOULD NOT reuse the selected network address before the client responds to the server's DHCPOFFER message
Not Recommended: SHOULD NOT reuse the selected network address before the client responds to the server's DHCPOFFER message

Mandatory: T1 MUST be earlier than T2
T2 MUST be earlier than the time at which the client's lease will expire
The client MUST NOT include a 'server identifier' in the DHCPREQUEST message
Any DHCPACK messages that arrive with an 'xid' that does not match the 'xid' of the client's DHCPREQUEST message are silently discarded

If the lease expires before the client receives a DHCPACK, the client moves to INIT state, MUST immediately stop any other network processing and requests network initialization parameters as if the client were uninitialized
If the client is given a new network address, it MUST NOT continue using the previous network address
Recommended: Times T1 and T2 SHOULD be chosen with some random "fuzz" around a fixed value, to avoid synchronization of client reacquisition
The server SHOULD return T1 and T2, and their values SHOULD be adjusted from their original values to take account of the time remaining on the lease

In both RENEWING and REBINDING states, if the client receives no response to its DHCPREQUEST message, the client SHOULD wait one-half of the remaining time until T2 (in RENEWING state) and one-half of the remaining lease time (in REBINDING state), down to a minimum of 60 seconds, before retransmitting the DHCPREQUEST message
If the client then receives a DHCPACK allocating that client its previous network address, the client SHOULD continue network processing
If the client is given a new network address, it MUST NOT continue using the previous network address and SHOULD notify the local users of the problem

Mandatory: MUST include the 'server identifier' option
MUST be set to the value of 'yiaddr' in the DHCPOFFER message from the server
MUST use the same value in the DHCP message header's 'secs' field
MUST be sent to the same IP broadcast address as the original DHCPDISCOVER message
MUST send a DHCPDECLINE message to the server
MUST use the same 'client identifier' in the DHCPRELEASE message
Recommended: SHOULD check that the offered network address is not already in use
SHOULD be implemented so that network administrators MAY choose to disable probes of newly allocated addresses
SHOULD NOT check the offered network address at this point
SHOULD NOT conflict with those in the earlier DHCPOFFER message
SHOULD respond with a DHCPNAK message
SHOULD mark an address offered to a client in a DHCPOFFER message as available if the server receives no DHCPREQUEST message from that client
SHOULD perform a final check on the parameters
SHOULD wait a minimum of ten seconds before restarting the configuration process
SHOULD notify the user that the initialization process has failed and is restarting
Not Recommended: SHOULD NOT conflict with those in the earlier DHCPOFFER message
SHOULD NOT check the offered network address at this point

Mandatory: The last option must always be the 'end' option
A server with multiple network addresses MUST be prepared to accept any of its network addresses as identifying that server in a DHCP message
To accommodate potentially incomplete network connectivity, a server MUST choose an address as a 'server identifier' that, to the best of the server's knowledge, is reachable from the client
DHCP clients MUST use the IP address provided in the 'server identifier' option for any unicast requests to the DHCP server
If the options in a DHCP message extend into the 'sname' and 'file' fields, the 'option overload' option MUST appear in the 'options' field
If the 'option overload' option is present in the 'options' field, the options in the 'options' field MUST be terminated by an 'end' option
The options in the 'sname' and 'file' fields (if in use as indicated by the 'option overload' option) MUST begin with the first octet of the field
The options in the 'sname' and 'file' fields (if in use as indicated by the 'option overload' option) MUST be terminated by an 'end' option
The options in the 'sname' and 'file' fields (if in use as indicated by the 'option overload' option) MUST be followed by 'pad' options to fill the remainder of the field
Any individual option in the 'options', 'sname' and 'file' fields MUST be entirely contained in that field
The options in the 'options' field MUST be interpreted first
The 'file' field MUST be interpreted next (if the 'option overload' option indicates that the 'file' field contains DHCP options)
DHCP clients are responsible for all message retransmission
The client MUST adopt a retransmission strategy that incorporates a randomized exponential backoff algorithm to determine the delay between retransmissions
The 'xid' field is used by the client to match incoming DHCP messages with pending requests
A DHCP client MUST choose 'xid's in such a way as to minimize the chance of using an 'xid' identical to one used by another client
Recommended: if the DHCP server and the DHCP client are connected to the same subnet (i.e., the 'giaddr' field in the message from the client is zero), the server SHOULD select the IP address the server is using for communication on that subnet as the 'server identifier'
If the server has received a message through a DHCP relay agent, the server SHOULD choose an address from the interface on which the message was received as the 'server identifier'

The delay between retransmissions SHOULD be chosen to allow sufficient time for replies from the server to be delivered based on the characteristics of the internetwork between the client and the server
For example, in a 10Mb/sec Ethernet internetwork, the delay before the first retransmission SHOULD be 4 seconds randomized by the value of a uniform random number chosen from the range -1 to +1
The delay before the next retransmission SHOULD be 8 seconds randomized by the value of a uniform number chosen from the range -1 to +1
The retransmission delay SHOULD be doubled with subsequent retransmissions up to a maximum of 64 seconds

A client that cannot receive unicast IP datagrams until its protocol software has been configured with an IP address SHOULD set the BROADCAST bit in the 'flags' field to 1 in any DHCPDISCOVER or DHCPREQUEST messages that client sends
A client that can receive unicast IP datagrams before its protocol software has been configured SHOULD clear the BROADCAST bit to 0
A server or relay agent sending or relaying a DHCP message directly to a DHCP client (i.e., not to a relay agent specified in the 'giaddr' field) SHOULD examine the BROADCAST bit in the 'flags' field

If this bit is set to 1, the DHCP message SHOULD be sent as an IP broadcast using an IP broadcast address (preferably 0xffffffff) as the IP destination address and the link-layer broadcast address as the link-layer destination address
If the BROADCAST bit is cleared to 0, the message SHOULD be sent as an IP unicast to the IP address specified in the 'yiaddr' field and the link-layer address specified in the 'chaddr' field
